skill.name: encode-api
skill.description: Access the ENCODE Project REST API to search for and retrieve biological data (biosamples, experiments, etc.). Use this skill when the user needs to query ENCODE data, search by keywords, or retrieve specific objects by accession ID.
skill.summary: Query the ENCODE REST API to search and retrieve biological data from the ENCODE Project database.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 61 potential issues, including weak crypto patterns and system reconnaissance indicators. After human evaluation, all HIGH and CRITICAL findings are dismissed as false positives. The skill makes HTTP requests to encodeproject.org (a legitimate NIH scientific database), accepts optional API credentials as function parameters, and includes shell command examples in documentation. No malicious intent or data exfiltration detected.

low_findings.title: Optional API credential parameters
low_findings.description: Functions accept api_key and api_secret as optional parameters for accessing private ENCODE data. Credentials are not hardcoded, stored, or exfiltrated. Public data does not require authentication.

low_findings.title: Shell command examples in documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains shell command examples for running the Python client. These are documentation instructions for the AI agent, not active code execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTTP requests to external API
dangerous_patterns.description: Python requests library used to make HTTP GET requests to encodeproject.org. Target is a legitimate NIH-funded scientific database. No data exfiltration to unknown endpoints.

dangerous_patterns.title: Optional credential propagation via auth tuple
dangerous_patterns.description: API credentials accepted as function parameters and passed to requests library as HTTP Basic Auth. Credentials only sent to encodeproject.org and only when user provides them.

content.user_title: Query ENCODE Project API for Biological Data
content.value_statement: Accessing the ENCODE biological database requires understanding complex REST API endpoints and authentication options. This skill simplifies searching for biosamples, experiments, and genomic data using ready-to-use Python functions.
actual_capabilities: Search the ENCODE database for biosamples, experiments, and annotations using keyword queries
Retrieve specific biological objects by their ENCODE accession ID
Support optional API key authentication for accessing private ENCODE data
Limit search results to control response size and focus output
Handle HTTP errors with clear feedback for failed requests
limitations: Requires internet access to connect to the ENCODE REST API
Limited to data available through the public ENCODE API at encodeproject.org
Does not include local data caching for offline access to previously retrieved data
Search terms are not URL-encoded in the current implementation

use_cases.title: Search for Biosamples by Keyword
use_cases.description: Find relevant biosamples by searching the ENCODE database with experimental terms.

use_cases.title: Retrieve Experiment Metadata by Accession
use_cases.description: Look up detailed metadata for a known experiment using its ENCODE accession ID.

use_cases.title: Access ENCODE Datasets Programmatically
use_cases.description: Integrate ENCODE data retrieval into automated analysis pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Basic ENCODE Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the ENCODE database for biosamples related to bone tissue.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to find biosamples by keyword

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve Object by Accession ID
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the ENCODE object with accession ID ENCBS000AAA and show its metadata.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a known accession ID and needs its details

prompt_templates.title: Customized Search with Limit
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ENCODE for experiments related to chromatin and return 5 results.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a targeted search with controlled result size

prompt_templates.title: Private Data with Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ENCODE for my private lab data using API key KEY_ID and secret SECRET_VALUE.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to access non-public ENCODE data with credentials

output_examples.input: Search the ENCODE database for biosamples related to bone tissue.
output_examples.output: A list of matching biosample entries with their accession IDs, types, and descriptions returned by the ENCODE API.

output_examples.input: Retrieve the ENCODE object with accession ID ENCBS000AAA.
output_examples.output: Detailed metadata for the object including its type, description, and related experimental information.

output_examples.input: Search ENCODE for experiments related to chromatin and return 3 results.
output_examples.output: A curated list of up to 3 experiment entries with accession IDs, experiment types, and brief descriptions.

best_practices: Start searches with a small result limit to verify relevance before retrieving large datasets
Provide specific and accurate search terms to get more focused results
Use the public API without authentication for publicly available data whenever possible
anti_patterns: Searching with overly broad terms that return thousands of irrelevant results
Passing API credentials when accessing public data that does not require authentication
Ignoring HTTP error feedback and continuing to request data that may not exist

faq.question: What is the ENCODE Project?
faq.answer: The ENCODE Project is a public research project that aims to identify functional elements in the human genome. Its data is freely available through the REST API at encodeproject.org.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. Public ENCODE data is accessible without any authentication. API keys are only needed for accessing private or restricted data.

faq.question: What kind of data can I retrieve?
faq.answer: You can retrieve biosamples, experiments, annotations, and other biological data available through the ENCODE Project REST API.

faq.question: Is there a rate limit on the ENCODE API?
faq.answer: Yes. The ENCODE API enforces a rate limit of 10 requests per second.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without internet access?
faq.answer: No. This skill requires internet access to connect to the ENCODE REST API. It does not provide offline data access.

faq.question: How are errors handled when the API fails?
faq.answer: HTTP errors are caught and displayed with descriptive messages. For example, a 404 error indicates the requested resource was not found.
